rulings.Case Title: DONALD L. BASS et al VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Exxon Mobil Corporation's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is continued on the court's own motion to June 24, 2014 due to the court's schedule. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-13-276204 WITH CASE CGC-11-275820
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiffs' motion for order to consolidate actions for all purposes including trial is granted. Case no. 275820 and case no. 276204 are consolidated for all purposes including trial. Case no. 275820 is the lead case. Case no. 275820's Trial Setting Conference date of August 21, 2014 is continued to October 16, 2014. Case no. 276204's Trial Setting Conference date of October 16, 2014 remains. No party opposes consolidation. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: PRATT, AVIS INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTER et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication As To Plaintiffs' First, Second, Fifth, And Sixth, Causes Of Action
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Mine Safety Appliances Co.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed May 30, 2014. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 12, 2014 in Department 503, Line 5. Defendant Kelsey-Hayes Company's motion for summary judgment or, alternatively, summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
